rag_rules: When repairing AGREE contracts, ensure that all identifiers used in assume/guarantee expressions are visible in the current AADL component scope and properly declared.
If a diagnostic reports unresolved references, do not introduce new declarations; instead, rewrite or delete the clause to use only existing visible identifiers.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must be placed in valid AADL scopes: component types for assumptions/guarantees, implementations for bindings.
Generated AGREE code must not introduce package-qualified identifiers or types not present in the model's configured dependencies.
AGREE syntax requires specific keywords: assume, guarantee, eq, const, assign, and expressions must match declared types.
Repair actions should preserve the existing architecture and avoid introducing new component features or connections.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Use only visible identifiers from the current AADL scope; avoid referencing undefined or package-qualified names.
Ensure type consistency: eq helpers and const declarations must use AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool).
anti_patterns: Avoid adding new AADL features or modifying component structure during AGREE repair; focus only on correcting contract expressions.
Do not attempt to resolve missing identifiers by inventing new declarations; instead, remove or rewrite clauses using visible identifiers.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE annex placement in component types vs. implementations
Component type and implementation structure in AADL
